source.title: Structural Damage - Mooring Line Failure, Diana Hoover Spar

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Alaminos Canyon Block 25

actions_taken: ROV survey of remaining 11 mooring lines performed on July 27, 2021
Failed chain link recovered from seafloor and sent to Stress Engineering Services for evaluation
Visual inspection and hardness testing conducted on accessible links from deck to waterline on remaining 11 lines
Chain locker entry performed with hardness testing on links adjacent to failed link
Facility Model analysis performed to confirm stability with 11 remaining mooring lines
event.summary: On July 18, 2021, the Diana Hoover Spar facility experienced loss of mooring line #4 due to chain link failure. The 160 mm R4 grade chain link, in service for approximately 20 years, fractured at the crown and flash weld. Investigation by Stress Engineering Services determined the fractures were caused by hydrogen embrittlement resulting from improper heat treatment during manufacturing, which resulted in high hardness, low ductility, and low impact toughness. The facility remained stable with the remaining 11 mooring lines per 2010 Facility Model analysis. Estimated repair cost is $10-15 million with 6-9 months required for replacement chain manufacturing.

threats.name: Improper heat treatment during chain manufacturing
High hardness and low toughness in chain material

controls.name: Facility Model analysis for mooring system performance
controls.barrier_role: Assess facility stability following mooring line loss
controls.lod_basis: Post-failure assessment to confirm safe continued operations

supporting_text: A Facility Model (performed in 2010) contained updated information on moorings, risers and Metocean Criteria
The model analysis also investigated multi-line failure scenarios which include mooring system performance (offsets and tensions) analysis for one and two lines failed
Based on the 2010 Facility Model, the Lessee determined the Facility was stable with the remaining eleven mooring lines and within safety standards to continue production operations

controls.name: ROV survey of mooring lines
controls.barrier_role: Detect anomalies in remaining mooring lines post-failure
controls.lod_basis: Post-incident inspection to identify additional defects

supporting_text: On July 27, 2021 a remotely operated vehicle (ROV) surveyed the 11 remaining mooring lines and reported no anomalies identified during the survey

controls.name: Visual inspection and hardness testing of mooring chain links
controls.barrier_role: Identify defective chain links susceptible to hydrogen embrittlement
controls.lod_basis: Post-incident material testing to assess remaining chain integrity

supporting_text: ExxonMobil conducted visual inspection and hardness testing on all accessible links from the AC25 SPAR deck to the waterline on the remaining 11 top chains which accounted for approximately 20 links per line
The resulting test results indicated zero out of tolerance readings according to ABS Grade 4 requirements

Additionally, chain locker #4 entry was performed, and hardness testing was conducted on the links adjacent to the failed link (up to 15 links away from failure) and each tested link exhibited high hardness

controls.name: Third-party metallurgical analysis of failed chain link
controls.barrier_role: Determine root cause of chain link failure
controls.lod_basis: Post-incident forensic investigation to understand failure mechanism

supporting_text: ExxonMobil recovered the broken link from the chain on the seafloor and sent the chain link to Stress Engineering Services, Inc. (SES) for evaluation

SES performed several tests on the chain link, including visual examination, wet fluorescent magnetic particle testing, dimensional measurements, fractography, metallography, hardness testing, and other material characterization tests
SES investigation report found that both the crown fracture and the weld fracture were associated with hydrogen embrittlement
The Material Characterization Tests found that the chain link properties did not satisfy ABS requirement for Grade R4 mooring chain, nor any other grade of mooring chain
The poor link properties are most likely the result of improper heat treatment during manufacturing, which resulted in low inherent toughness and increased susceptibility to hydrogen embrittlement

controls.name: Operator detection of mooring line failure
controls.barrier_role: Detect mooring line loss through facility monitoring

supporting_text: While conducting normal production operations on the Spar Facility, the Operators heard a loud rumble noise and felt the Facility shake slightly
The Operators surveyed the Facility and discovered the number four mooring line parted

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
